title: artemisia rupestris subsp. viridis
intro: Artemisia rupestris subsp. viridis, also known as rock wormwood, is a subspecies of the wormwood plant, native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral agent. The health benefits of rock wormwood include its potential to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and support the immune system. Rock wormwood can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some traditional remedies for its purported ability to ward off insects and other pests.
